title: Hierarchy of Algorithmic Solvability
description: A flowchart illustrating the boundaries of what algorithms can and cannot solve, categorizing problems based on their solvability and complexity.
relationships: P is a subset of NP
NP-Complete problems are the hardest problems in NP
Undecidable problems have no algorithm that can always provide a correct yes/no answer
Uncomputable functions have no algorithm that can compute them for all inputs
